etymology.description: 爪(爪) (zhǎo, "hand") + 又(又) (yòu, "hand") + 石(石) (shí, "stone") = two hands handling a stone for borrowing -> phonetic usage derived from original concept
etymology.interpretation: The character 叚 (jiǎ, "phonetic") originates from a depiction of two hands interacting with a stone, illustrating the original notion of borrowing; however, it was later used mainly for its phonetic value.
